title: Code of Conduct Lesson #4 — Youth Soccer Referee Abuse (Level 4: Discrimination)
source: Based on U.S. Soccer Policy 531-9, effective March 2025

questions.question: What makes Level 4 Abuse distinct from Levels 1–3 under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: It only applies to physical contact that causes injury

answers.label: B
answers.text: It attacks who the referee IS — their identity — rather than what they decided on the field

answers.label: C
answers.text: It requires a prior Level 3 offense before it can be charged

answers.label: D
answers.text: It applies only to professional matches, not youth soccer

questions.question: What is the minimum suspension for a Level 4 discriminatory offense under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: 6 games plus 6–12 months

answers.label: B
answers.text: 10 games plus 12–24 months

answers.label: C
answers.text: 12 games plus 6–24 months

answers.label: D
answers.text: Immediate lifetime ban for any first offense

questions.question: Does a racial or ethnic slur only violate Policy 531-9 if it is shouted loudly in front of witnesses?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Yes — the slur must be audible to others to constitute an offense

answers.label: B
answers.text: Yes — it must be caught on video to be penalized

answers.label: C
answers.text: No — it applies whether spoken on the field, from the sideline, or said under one's breath

answers.label: D
answers.text: Only if the referee directly reports hearing it

questions.question: Which of the following is explicitly listed as a protected characteristic under Policy 531-9 Level 4?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A referee's years of experience

answers.label: B
answers.text: A referee's assignment to a specific match

answers.label: C
answers.text: A referee's nationality, social origin, language, and religion

answers.label: D
answers.text: A referee's uniform color or badge level

questions.question: A spectator mocks a referee's accent to discredit a call. Under Policy 531-9, this is:

answers.label: A
answers.text: Level 2 Abuse — Personal Attack

answers.label: B
answers.text: Level 1 Abuse — Taunting Behavior

answers.label: C
answers.text: Level 4 Abuse — Discriminatory Language Based on National Origin or Language

answers.label: D
answers.text: Not covered — accent-based comments are not explicitly named in the policy

questions.question: Saying a female referee 'doesn't understand the game' to question her call is classified as what under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Level 1 Abuse — Questioning Competence

answers.label: B
answers.text: An opinion about officiating quality, not covered by the policy

answers.label: C
answers.text: Level 4 Abuse — Harmful or Sexist Stereotype

answers.label: D
answers.text: Level 2 Abuse — Personal Attack

questions.question: Does Policy 531-9's Level 4 cover only spoken words, or also non-verbal discriminatory acts?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Only spoken words are covered

answers.label: B
answers.text: Only written communications such as social media posts

answers.label: C
answers.text: Both words and non-verbal discriminatory acts, including gestures and physical expressions

answers.label: D
answers.text: Only actions that involve physical contact with the referee

questions.question: How does Policy 531-9 evaluate a discriminatory gesture directed at a referee?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Gestures are not penalized unless accompanied by verbal abuse

answers.label: B
answers.text: The policy evaluates the meaning and intent of the gesture, not just the medium

answers.label: C
answers.text: Gestures carry a lesser penalty than spoken slurs

answers.label: D
answers.text: Only gestures made on the field of play are covered

questions.question: Casually calling a referee 'blind' in a context that targets a perceived disability — rather than expressing frustration — is classified as what?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Acceptable venting — the policy distinguishes between frustration and discrimination

answers.label: B
answers.text: Level 1 Abuse — Mocking Abilities

answers.label: C
answers.text: Level 4 Abuse — Discriminatory Language Based on Disability

answers.label: D
answers.text: Level 2 Abuse — Degrading Language

questions.question: What is the penalty for a second Level 4 offense under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The same 10-game minimum as the first offense

answers.label: B
answers.text: An automatic lifetime ban

answers.label: C
answers.text: 20 games plus an extended time ban

answers.label: D
answers.text: 15 games plus a 12-month ban

questions.question: What is the consequence for a third Level 4 offense?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A 2-year suspension with a reinstatement review

answers.label: B
answers.text: Automatic lifetime ban from all U.S. Soccer-affiliated activity as participant or spectator

answers.label: C
answers.text: Referral to a national disciplinary panel for a custom ruling

answers.label: D
answers.text: A 30-game suspension plus a 24-month ban

questions.question: What automatic penalty multiplier applies when a Level 4 offense targets a referee who is under 18?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Double the standard penalty

answers.label: B
answers.text: Triple the standard penalty

answers.label: C
answers.text: The standard penalty applies — age is not a factor at Level 4

answers.label: D
answers.text: An additional 12-month ban is added to the standard suspension

questions.question: Beyond the soccer penalty, what broader legal risk does the course identify for discriminatory conduct?

answers.label: A
answers.text: No legal risk — Policy 531-9 is the only applicable framework

answers.label: B
answers.text: Only liability if the discrimination is recorded on video

answers.label: C
answers.text: Discriminatory conduct in public settings may violate civil rights law depending on jurisdiction and circumstances

answers.label: D
answers.text: Legal risk applies only if the offender is a paid league official

questions.question: According to the course, what message does discriminatory sideline behavior send to young players from the same background as the targeted referee?

answers.label: A
answers.text: That the game has high standards everyone must meet

answers.label: B
answers.text: That this game may not be for you

answers.label: C
answers.text: That referees need thicker skin to work in competitive environments

answers.label: D
answers.text: That passion for the sport can sometimes go too far

questions.question: What does the course recommend if you hear another spectator make a discriminatory comment at a youth match?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Ignore it — it is not your responsibility to police other adults

answers.label: B
answers.text: Record it on your phone and post it online

answers.label: C
answers.text: Speak up — say something like 'That's not okay here' and report it to the league in writing

answers.label: D
answers.text: Alert the referee immediately so they can issue a formal warning
